title: SSRF starts with a helpful URL box
hook: The feature says "paste a URL and we will fetch it for you." The attacker hears "make our server talk to places your browser cannot reach."

beats.heading: Fetching a URL moves trust to your server

blocks.html: URL importers are useful: unfurl a link, fetch an avatar, import a feed, preview a webhook. But once your backend makes the request, it has a different network position than the user. It may reach internal hosts, metadata services, admin panels, or private IP ranges.

blocks.caption: This helper is one validation step away from becoming a tunnel.
Security gets real when the trust boundaries light up. Poke it and see what has to hold.

blocks.html: The attacker does not need your credentials if they can make your server ask the question. They try localhost, private address ranges, cloud metadata endpoints, redirects, DNS tricks, and alternate URL forms. The server becomes the browser they wanted.

blocks.title: zsh · attacker input
blocks.caption: Do not fetch first and validate later.

lines.t: backend fetches from inside the network

beats.heading: Validate, resolve, and restrict the egress path

blocks.html: A strong defense is layered: allow only expected schemes, parse with a real URL parser, resolve DNS, block private and link-local ranges, re-check after redirects, limit response size and timeout, and send fetches through an egress proxy that enforces the policy.

blocks.title: security control lab
blocks.caption: Flip the switches and watch the story turn into a tiny operating model.
blocks.low: exposed: the feature trusts too much and records too little
blocks.mid: better: the easy abuse paths now have friction
blocks.high: hardened: authorization, limits, and recovery all have a job

items.label: schemes usually enough: http and https

beats.heading: Treat server-side fetch as a privileged action

blocks.html: The mindset shift is the whole game. A URL box is not just input validation. It is a network access decision. Put it through the same review you would give a new outbound integration.
